name: Embalse del Molino de Chincha Trail
name_original: Running por la senda del Embalse del Molino de Chincha
description: Jog the moderate 6.2-mile Embalse del Molino de Chincha Trail, enjoying reservoir views and pine forests in Alto Tajo Natural Park.
region_intro: Jogging around Cañizares offers an opportunity to explore the diverse landscapes of the Cuenca Mountains within the Serranía Alta Conquense. The region features impressive peaks, verdant terrain, and deep gorges, providing varied surfaces for running. Jogging routes in Cañizares often lead through natural surroundings, offering both physical challenge and scenic views. The area's trails are suitable for those seeking to combine their run with an exploration of the local natural environment. Best jogging routes around Cañizares The most popular jogging route is Sendero del Cucurucho y la Hocecilla – circular trail run from Fuentescusa, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging circular trail run starting from Fuentescusa. Another top favourite among local runners is Dehesa de los Olmos Trail, a difficult 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This trail provides an extensive running experience through the Dehesa de los Olmos. Local runners also love the Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – running circular by sendero del Cañizar, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 48 minutes. Jogging around Cañizares is defined by mountainous terrain, deep gorges, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Cañizares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Cañizares?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Cañizares, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These trails allow you to explore the diverse landscapes of the Cuenca Mountains within the Serranía Alta Conquense.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Cañizares?
A: Jogging routes in Cañizares feature a mix of mountainous terrain, deep gorges, and verdant landscapes. You'll encounter varied surfaces, from natural trails through lush forests to paths leading to panoramic viewpoints like the Alto de Peñajosa, offering both physical challenge and stunning scenery.

Q: Are there any moderate running trails suitable for intermediate joggers?
A: Yes, Cañizares offers several moderate running trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014672043/cascada-campana-del-tio-mil-hombres-running-circular-by-sendero-del-canizar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada Campana del Tío Mil Hombres – running circular by sendero del Cañizar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.1-mile route. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014666214/embalse-del-molino-de-chincha-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Molino de Chincha Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is nearly 10 miles long and also rated moderate.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Cañizares area?
A: Absolutely! Many of the running routes around Cañizares are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014669063/sendero-del-cucurucho-y-la-hocecilla-circular-trail-run-from-fuentescusa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero del Cucurucho y la Hocecilla – circular trail run from Fuentescusa&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 7.6-mile loop. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014375947/hoz-somera-and-castillejos-circular-trail-run-from-carrascosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz Somera and Castillejos – circular trail run from Carrascosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting natural attractions can I see along the running routes?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can discover stunning waterfalls like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuervo River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5404050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Hocino Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. For breathtaking views, many routes lead to impressive peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5411820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peñajosa&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cucurucho Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options for experienced runners?
A: Yes, experienced trail runners will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014668196/dehesa-de-los-olmos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa de los Olmos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.9-mile route offering an extensive running experience. Many routes in the Cuenca Mountains feature significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, providing a demanding workout.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Cañizares?
A: The Cuenca Mountains, where Cañizares is located, offer pleasant conditions for jogging during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer mornings and evenings can also be suitable, but it's best to avoid the midday heat. Winter can be beautiful, but some higher elevation trails might be more challenging due to colder weather or potential snow.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its spectacular viewpoints. Routes leading towards elevated points like the Alto de Peñajosa provide wonderful panoramas of the Cuenca mountain range, the impressive Escabas river gorge, and the Dehesa de Los Olmos. These offer rewarding visual experiences after a challenging ascent.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging trails in Cañizares?
A: The jogging trails around Cañizares are popular among the komoot community, with over 50 runners having explored the varied terrain. Users often praise the stunning natural beauty, the challenging yet rewarding ascents to viewpoints, and the opportunity to immerse oneself in the wild nature of the Serranía Alta Conquense.

Q: Are there any specific routes that pass by a reservoir or water body?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014666214/embalse-del-molino-de-chincha-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Molino de Chincha Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.2-mile route that takes you around the Embalse del Molino de Chincha, offering scenic views of the reservoir.
tips: Upon arrival at the Strait of Guijarro, the Guadiela River was channeled forming the Molino de Chincha reservoir, a paradise with turquoise waters that can be traveled along its eastern bank, an itinerary with beautiful panoramic views in every corner.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows a well-maintained track, making it comfortable and easy for jogging. It is predominantly flat, with a significant portion running under the shade of a dense pine forest. You'll find sections bordering the reservoir's turquoise waters and some elevated points offering views.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse del Molino de Chincha Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered to have a low or easy difficulty level, making it suitable for beginners and a wide range of fitness levels. While some sources mention sections requiring steady footing, it's largely accessible and comfortable for jogging.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll enjoy continuous panoramic views of the turquoise waters of the Molino de Chincha Reservoir. You can also spot geological formations like Hoz Somera and Los Castillejos Rock Formation. The path follows the Guadiela River, and you'll pass the dam of the Embalse del Molino de Chincha, built in 1947.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is especially recommended during warmer months due to the ample shade provided by the dense pine trees. This makes it a pleasant option even when temperatures are higher.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse del Molino de Chincha Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed, it is located within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Generally, dogs are permitted in natural parks but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail connects the town of Puente de Vadillos with the dam. It's advisable to look for parking options in or near Puente de Vadillos, which serves as a common starting point for accessing the route.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Alto Tajo Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Embalse del Molino de Chincha Trail or the Alto Tajo Natural Park itself for general recreational use like jogging. However, always respect park regulations and signage.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route largely follows the Ruta del Embalse del Molino de Chincha. It also briefly intersects sections of the [CIMA CU10] Belvalle * Puente de Vadillos and cno. de Carrascosa a Vadillos.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The surrounding landscape is rich in flora and fauna, characteristic of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. While specific sightings vary, you can expect to see diverse plant life, especially within the pine forests, and potentially various bird species and smaller mammals typical of the region.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Embalse del Molino de Chincha Trail is primarily described as a linear route, connecting Puente de Vadillos with the dam. This suggests it is an out-and-back trail, meaning you would return along the same path.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: For an average jogger, the full 9.96 km route typically takes around 1 hour and 12 minutes to complete.
